text:
Once upon an autumn evening, young Lily went out walking alone. She loved exploring dark forests. Tonight, she thought maybe an owl would chat.

As she sniffed here and there. she found straw yellow. Maybe an owl feather? She stooped low.

Oww.

An owl owl.

Hinky.

A twig snap.

She turned slow.

Just an owl.

She’d found her feather.

Why was I so silly?

She’d carry it.

It’d charm an owl.

She thought.

Like my hair.

She took photos.

Sunrise. No feather.

She’d buy stars.

Anyway.

It wouldnastick here.

Next morning.

I found one blue.

I kept.

Now an owl owl.

I sing.

I soar.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
